content: The vapour pressure of pure $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2$$ are 200 and $$41.5 \mathrm{~atm}$$ respectively. The weight of $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2$$ are respectively $$11.9 \mathrm{~g}$$ and $$17 \mathrm{~g}$$. The vapour pressure of solution will be
explanation:
Given,

Vapour pressure of pure $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3\left[p_1\right]$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2\left[p_2\right]=200 \mathrm{~atm}$$ and $$41.5 \mathrm{~atm}$$ respectively. Weight $$\left(w_1\right)$$ of $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2\left(w_2\right)=11.9 \mathrm{~g}$$ and $$17 \mathrm{~g}$$ respectively. Molar mass of $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3\left(M_1\right)$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2\left(M_2\right)=119 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ and $$85 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$ respectively.

Thus, number of moles $$(n)$$ of $$\mathrm{CHCl}_3=\frac{11.9}{119}=0.1 \mathrm{~mol}$$

Number of moles of $$\mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2=\frac{17}{85}=0.2 \mathrm{~mol}$$

$$\therefore p_{\text {total }}=p_1 \chi_1+p_2 \chi_2$$

$$\begin{aligned} \chi_1 & =\frac{n_1}{n_T}=\frac{0.1}{0.3}=0.33 \\ \chi_2 & =\frac{n_2}{n_T}=\frac{0.2}{0.3}=0.66 \\ p_{\text {Total }} & =200 \times 0.33+41.5 \times 0.66=94.33 \end{aligned}$$

Hence, option (c) is the correct.

content: If boiling point of water is $$100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$$. How much gram of $$\mathrm{NaCl}$$ is added in $$500 \mathrm{~g}$$ of water to increase its boiling point of water by approx $$1^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\left(K_b\right)_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}=0.52 \mathrm{~K} \times \mathrm{kg} /$$ mole.
explanation:
Key idea For the solutes, which can undergo association or dissociation in a given solvent. Elevation in boiling point $$\left(\Delta T_b\right)=i K_b \times m$$, where, $$K_b=$$ molar elevation constant $$m=$$ molality, $$i=$$ van't Hoff factor

Given,

$$\begin{aligned} & \Delta T_b=1^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \text { and } M(\mathrm{NaCl})=58.5 \\ & K_b\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)=0.52 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol} \\ & \text { for } \mathrm{NaCl}=n=(2) \text { and } w_{\text {solvent }}=500 \mathrm{~g} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text{Thus,} \quad \Delta T_b & =i \times K_b \times \frac{w(\text { solute })}{M(\text { solute })} \times \frac{1000}{w(\text { solvent })} \\ 1^{\circ} & =2 \times 0.52 \times \frac{w}{58.5} \times \frac{1000}{500} \\ w & =\frac{58.5 \times 500}{2 \times 0.52 \times 1000}=28.125 \mathrm{~g} \end{aligned}$$

Hence, option (b) is the correct.

content: Benzoic acid undergoes dimerisation in benzene solution. The van't Hoff factor $$(i)$$ is related to the degree of association '$$x$$' of the acid as
explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \text { Total }=1-x+\frac{x}{2}=1-\frac{x}{2} \\\\ & \therefore \quad i=\frac{1-x / 2}{1} \\\\ \text{or,} & i=1-\frac{x}{2} \\\\ \end{aligned}$$
